steps.title: Identify the key contrast between the traditions
steps.content: The student wants to make a distinction between portolan traditions. The notes establish one defining contrast: Majorcan charts were “richly illustrated” while Venetian ones were “sparse in illustrations.”

steps.title: Select the choice that uses this distinction effectively
steps.content: Choice A directly contrasts the two traditions' illustration styles—Majorcan “richly illustrated,” Venetian “sparse”—to accomplish the goal. The other three either fail to specify the actual difference between traditions (B), conflate universal characteristics with tradition-specific ones (C), or misattribute features to the wrong tradition (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
